name: Agent Librairy
description: The agents directory is the core execution engine of the AI Lean Factory. It contains a suite of specialized, self-contained Python packages that perform discrete tasks in the app generation and validation pipeline.

name: Code Library
description: The Code directory houses reusable code components (Python and Swift) packaged as self-contained "blocks," as well as local web UIs for exercising and testing these blocks.

name: iOS Ready
description: This document outlines the architecture and current state of the iOS ready models within the ai-lean-factory workspace, specifically focusing on the libraries/ai-farm/ready directory and its related registry files.

name: Knowledge Librairy
description: The libraries/knowledge directory serves as the centralized memory and vertical corpora for the AI Lean Factory. It contains domain-specific knowledge bases that various applications, design agents, and computer vision models draw upon. Instead of hardcoding logic, the system feeds these markdown-based references to agents to ground their decision-making in established theories, guidelines, and rules.

name: MCP Library
description: The MCP (Model Context Protocol) library houses shared servers and tools designed to be called by autonomous agents (such as Codex or Claude). Each subdirectory represents a standalone MCP server with its own operational bounds, run instructions, and internal state management.

name: Model Library
description: This directory houses the AI-Lean-Factory's vision-model layer. It is built around a "one model = one self-contained block" architecture to ensure that models are highly portable, easily testable in CI environments, and trivially wrappable as MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools.

name: Skills
description: The libraries directory is the designated central repository for reusable, cross-app skills within the AI Lean Factory project. A "skill" represents a focused, specific capability that AI agents can utilize to perform concrete tasks.
